text.title: Contract-First Development Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying contract-first-development. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix keyed to the methodology.

antipattern.symptom: Agent modifies server code first, then back-fills the OpenAPI spec to match the implementation.
antipattern.root-cause: No CI gate enforcing spec-before-code; "spec follows code" pattern becomes normal.
antipattern.fix: Add a pre-merge CI job that fails if any path in the diff is server code without an accompanying spec change. Invert the flow: spec edits only, until spec is committed.

antipattern.symptom: Hand-editing generated server stubs "just this once" instead of modifying the spec and regenerating.
antipattern.root-cause: Regenerate step is slow or destructive; engineer chooses local edit as a shortcut.
antipattern.fix: Generated dirs are in .gitignore-style exclusion; regen runs in CI from spec on every build. Local edits are wiped on next build, making the antipattern self-correcting.

antipattern.symptom: Copy-pasting schema definitions across paths instead of using $ref to components/schemas.
antipattern.root-cause: Author unfamiliar with $ref; generator output reads cleaner with inlined types until duplication compounds.
antipattern.fix: Add spectral rule `no-inline-schemas` rejecting inlined object types in path operations. Force every type ≥3 properties to live in components/schemas.

antipattern.symptom: Breaking change ships without consumer notification — clients crash on deploy.
antipattern.root-cause: No oasdiff gate; spec edits land alongside server changes with no detection.
antipattern.fix: Wire oasdiff into the pre-merge CI: fail on any ERR-level breaking change unless the commit body carries `BREAKING-CHANGE:` token. MAJOR version bump becomes mandatory before merge.
